titleStmt.title: Letter from Annie Curran to Nancy O'Rahilly, 10 May 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Annie Curran

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: his is a letter of condolence from Annie Curran to Nancy O'Rahilly (1875-1961). The writer expresses her sympathy and that of her family and the people of Ventry for Mrs. O'Rahilly on the death of her husband.Nancy O'Rahilly was a member of the Provisional Council of Cumann na mBan at the founding in 1914 and the wife of Michael Joseph O'Rahilly, self-described as 'The' O'Rahilly (1875-1916). The O'Rahilly fought in the Easter Rising and was shot in Parnell/Moore Street on 28 April 1916 and died some time later. Nancy was pregnant at the time of her husband's death – the child was born three months later.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ventry. May 10th 1916 Dear Mrs. O'Rahilly, It is with feelings of
deepest regret we learned of
the death of your dear noble
husband. May God & His Blessed
Mother comfort you & those dear
to you in this you bitter hour
of trial. My father, mother, brothers
& sisters join with me in sending
this sincerest sympathy. Yours in deepest sympathy Annie Curran P.S. the people of Ventry express
their sorrow for your trouble.
